title: Sliding Window Attention for Long-Context Transformers
abstract: We propose a fixed sliding-window attention pattern that reduces the O(n^2) memory cost of self-attention to O(nw), where w is the window size. Empirically, w=512 captures most local dependencies but loses long-range coreference signals on books and code.

title: BigBird: Block-Sparse Attention with Random and Global Tokens
abstract: BigBird combines local windows, random keys, and global tokens to approximate full attention with linear cost. The random keys provide a probabilistic guarantee on long-range information flow, but the chosen sparsity is fixed at training time and does not adapt to head-level specialisation.

title: Learned Token Selection for Efficient Long-Context Inference
abstract: We train a lightweight scorer that selects the top-m most relevant keys per query at inference time. Across 8 long-context benchmarks, our method matches dense attention with 12% of the FLOPs but requires careful warmup to avoid degenerate selections during early training.
